title: BCA protein standard curve (96-well)
description: Thermo Pierce BCA microplate layout: the 9-point BSA standard set (2000-0 ug/mL, vials A-I) in triplicate across the top rows, unknown protein samples below. Also works for Bradford.

payload.description: Protein-quantification standard curve following the Thermo Pierce BCA Protein Assay microplate procedure (https://documents.thermofisher.com/TFS-Assets/LSG/manuals/MAN0011430_Pierce_BCA_Protein_Asy_UG.pdf). Rows A-C hold the 9-point diluted-BSA standard set in triplicate: columns 1-8 = 2000, 1500, 1000, 750, 500, 250, 125, 25 ug/mL (Pierce vials A-H) and column 9 = 0 ug/mL blank (vial I). Rows D-H hold unknown samples. Microplate procedure: 25 uL sample + 200 uL working reagent (Reagent A:B 50:1), mix, 37 C for 30 min, read at 562 nm; working range 20-2000 ug/mL. The same map works for a Bradford assay (substitute the Bradford standard range).

region_labels.notes: Columns 1-8 = 2000, 1500, 1000, 750, 500, 250, 125, 25 ug/mL BSA (Pierce vials A-H), in triplicate down rows A-C. 25 uL per well + 200 uL working reagent, 37 C 30 min, read 562 nm.
Pierce vial I, diluent only. Subtract the mean A562 of these wells from every standard and sample reading.
Spare wells (columns 10-12, rows A-C). Use for extra standard or sample replicates.
Unknown protein samples, 25 uL each, in duplicate or triplicate. Keep readings within the 20-2000 ug/mL working range; dilute over-range samples and re-assay.
